title: Browser vs Search Engine
drDigitalIntro: Safari is your browser — it's the app that shows you websites. Google is a search engine — it helps you find websites. Think of Safari like a car, and Google like a map. You drive the car (Safari) and use the map (Google) to figure out where to go. You can use different search engines inside Safari: Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo. But they all work inside Safari.

playgroundTask.goal: Use a search engine inside your browser

steps.say: This whole window is Safari — your browser. Google is a search engine you visit inside it. Type google.com in the address bar and press Enter.
Now use the search engine to find something. Type apple pie recipe in the search box and press Enter.

drDigitalSuccess: You used Google (a search engine) inside Safari (a browser). The browser shows websites; the search engine helps you find them.
drDigitalHint: First go to google.com in the address bar. Then type your search into the search box on the Google page.
